prerequisites: A Phyllo account registered on the Phyllo Developer Dashboard (dashboard.getphyllo.com).
A client_id and client_secret issued from the dashboard's API credentials page.
A target environment value (Phyllo provides a sandbox for testing; production access is granted after requesting it through the dashboard).
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to begin extracting data.

authMethods.name: Client ID and secret (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Phyllo authenticates each API request with HTTP Basic auth. The connector's client_id and client_secret are combined as client_id:client_secret, base64-encoded, and sent in the Authorization header. Both values are obtained from the Phyllo Developer Dashboard.

setupSteps.title: Register on the Phyllo Developer Dashboard
setupSteps.body: Sign up at dashboard.getphyllo.com. Your Client ID and Client Secret are shown in the dashboard so you can copy them for the connector.

setupSteps.title: Choose your environment
setupSteps.body: Phyllo offers a sandbox for testing with mock data and a production environment for live creator data. Set the connector's environment field accordingly. Production access is granted only after you request it through the dashboard, so begin with the sandbox if you have not yet been approved.

setupSteps.title: Provide the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter client_id and client_secret from the dashboard. The connector uses these for HTTP Basic auth on every Phyllo API request, so make sure they match the selected environment.

setupSteps.title: Set the extraction start date
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest date from which the connector should pull records. Data created before this date is excluded from the sync.

sources.title: Getting started with Phyllo APIs (official docs)
Use Phyllo with Postman to access creator data (Postman blog)
